skill.name: agent-management
skill.description: Design, create, and update Claude Code subagents with focused roles, scoped tools, model choices, and reusable configuration files.
skill.summary: Create and maintain Claude Code subagents with clear roles and controlled tool access.
skill.author: Emz1998

security_audit.summary: Static analysis found many command, filesystem, and weak-crypto indicators, but review shows the command and crypto hits are Markdown documentation false positives. The real risk is medium because the skill creates persistent .claude/agents files and can configure broad tool permissions that affect future Claude Code behavior.

medium_findings.title: Persistent Subagent Configuration Writes
medium_findings.description: The skill writes or updates Claude Code subagent files under .claude/agents. This is the stated purpose, but those files can influence future agent behavior and should be reviewed before use.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines directly describe writing agent configuration files and document persistent project and user agent locations. The behavior is intentional and useful, but it changes future assistant behavior.

medium_findings.title: Broad Tool Permission Configuration Requires Review
medium_findings.description: The skill is designed to select tool permissions for generated agents. Documentation notes that omitted tools can inherit broad access, including file, shell, web, and MCP tools when available.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The tool-permission behavior is clearly documented and is central to the skill. The risk depends on the generated agent configuration, so this is a medium-risk governance concern rather than confirmed abuse.

low_findings.title: External Command Findings Are Documentation False Positives
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged Markdown code fences, inline command examples, and path examples as Ruby or shell backtick execution. No executable script or dynamic command execution was found in the skill files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited content is Markdown documentation and Claude Code slash command syntax. I found no code path that executes these strings as shell commands.

low_findings.title: Hidden Directory Findings Match Stated Claude Code Storage
low_findings.description: The .claude paths are documented storage locations for subagent Markdown files. This is filesystem access, but it matches the skill purpose and is not evidence of evasion or unauthorized persistence.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The hidden-directory references are explicit Claude Code agent locations, not concealed malware paths. The remaining concern is user review of generated files, which is captured as medium risk.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Findings Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The cited weak-crypto locations contain agent descriptions, model fields, Markdown examples, or template placeholders. No cryptographic operation, hash algorithm, or credential handling was found at those locations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review of the cited lines showed no cryptographic API, weak algorithm, encoded payload, or secret handling. The static signal appears to be triggered by ordinary documentation text.

dangerous_patterns.title: Persistent Assistant Behavior Configuration
dangerous_patterns.description: Generated subagent files can persist in project or user Claude Code agent directories and influence future delegation behavior.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The file write locations and persistence model are directly documented. This is expected behavior but important for marketplace users to understand.

dangerous_patterns.title: Configurable Access to Powerful Tools
dangerous_patterns.description: Generated agents may be configured with tools such as Bash, Write, WebFetch, and MCP tools. Excessive permissions can expand the impact of a flawed or overly broad agent prompt.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The cited files list powerful tools and describe how subagents receive tool access. The risk is conditional on user choices and generated configuration quality.

content.value_statement: Teams need consistent subagents without overbroad prompts or tool access. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code users through focused agent design, configuration, and updates.
actual_capabilities: Designs Claude Code subagents with clear roles and concise responsibilities.
Updates existing agent configurations with new capabilities or revised scope.
Selects model tiers based on task complexity and expected reasoning needs.
Chooses scoped tool permissions for each generated subagent.
Uses a consistent Markdown and YAML frontmatter template for agent files.
Stores generated agents in the appropriate .claude/agents folder.
limitations: It does not validate generated agents through a live Claude Code runtime.
It does not guarantee that selected tool permissions are safe for every repository.
It depends on the user to review generated agent files before committing them.
It is focused on Claude Code subagents, not general multi-agent orchestration.

use_cases.title: Create a focused code review agent
use_cases.description: Define a reusable reviewer with scoped read and search tools for project pull requests.

use_cases.title: Standardize team agent templates
use_cases.description: Build consistent subagent files that follow shared naming, model, and permission rules.

use_cases.title: Refine an existing specialist agent
use_cases.description: Update responsibilities, constraints, and tool access when a project workflow changes.

prompt_templates.title: Create a basic subagent
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for a new agent.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a Claude Code subagent for reviewing frontend changes. Keep the role focused and use only necessary tools.

prompt_templates.title: Update an existing subagent
prompt_templates.scenario: Revise an agent after workflow changes.
prompt_templates.prompt: Update the existing test-runner subagent so it can handle integration tests and report failures clearly.

prompt_templates.title: Design a permission-scoped agent
prompt_templates.scenario: Create an agent with explicit tool limits.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a database query helper subagent. Explain which tools it needs and avoid broad shell access unless required.

prompt_templates.title: Build a team agent set
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request for multiple coordinated agents.
prompt_templates.prompt: Design three Claude Code subagents for code review, debugging, and release checks. Avoid overlapping responsibilities and document each file location.

output_examples.input: Create a security review subagent for backend changes.
output: A proposed agent name and purpose.
A concise list of responsibilities and review steps.
Recommended tools limited to reading, searching, and inspection.
A target .claude/agents folder for the new file.

output_examples.input: Update the debugger agent to support API incidents.
output: A revised role summary for API debugging.
Updated workflow phases for reproducing and isolating failures.
A note about any changed tool permissions.
A completion summary with the updated agent location.

output_examples.input: Create agents for a frontend quality workflow.
output: Separate agent roles for accessibility review, component testing, and UI debugging.
Model recommendations based on task complexity.
Review guidance before the files are used by the team.

best_practices: Review every generated .claude/agents file before using it in a project.
Grant only the tools required for the agent purpose.
Keep each subagent focused on one role with measurable success criteria.
anti_patterns: Do not omit tool permissions when the agent should have limited access.
Do not create overlapping agents with similar names and responsibilities.
Do not use broad proactive descriptions for agents that should run only on request.

faq.question: What does this skill create?
faq.answer: It creates or updates Claude Code subagent Markdown files with YAML frontmatter, role guidance, workflow steps, and tool permissions.

faq.question: Does it run the generated agents?
faq.answer: No. It prepares agent configuration files and reports their location. Runtime behavior depends on Claude Code.

faq.question: Why is tool permission review important?
faq.answer: Subagents can receive powerful tools. Users should confirm that each agent has only the tools needed for its role.

faq.question: Can it update existing agents?
faq.answer: Yes. The workflow includes checking whether an agent exists and revising its configuration when appropriate.

faq.question: Where are agents stored?
faq.answer: The skill targets .claude/agents folders, including project-level folders and team-specific subfolders.

faq.question: Is this skill safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes, with a medium warning. The audit found no malicious intent, but persistent agent files and tool permissions require user review.
